decision: Reduce DfT/Secretary of State confusion risk to controlled P1 while retaining Bristol and WECA/MCA P0 blockers
options_considered: Leave all three Stage 2 legal/governance risks as P0; close all Stage 2 legal/governance risks; narrow only the confirmation-route distinction
rationale: Transport Act 2000 section 184 and section 198 evidence controls the Secretary of State confirmation route for initial England-only WPL schemes, but Bristol order-maker/submitter and WECA/MCA role remain unresolved

decision: Narrow Bristol authority-status risk while retaining internal order-maker and submitter P0 blocker
options_considered: Close Bristol route entirely; leave broad Bristol authority uncertainty unchanged; narrow only authority-status source chain
rationale: Transport Act 2000 sections 178 179 198 and 163 RTRA 1984 section 121A and Avon structural-change evidence support Bristol-led authority-status working control but do not allocate internal WPL order-making or statutory submission

decision: Control Bristol OBC progression and FBC member route while retaining final order-maker and submitter P0 blocker
options_considered: Reduce all Bristol internal route issues to P1; leave Bristol route entirely P0; narrow OBC progression and FBC route only
rationale: Bristol decision notices forward plans and constitution sources support Transport and Connectivity Committee route for OBC progression and FBC but do not expressly allocate final WPL licensing-scheme order-making or Secretary of State submission

decision: Control WECA/MCA evidence language while retaining current-law role and funding dependency P0 blocker
options_considered: Close WECA/MCA role; leave all WECA/MCA evidence unclassified; narrow only strategic-context and conditional funding-assurance wording
rationale: WECA strategy assurance constitution and current-law sources support strategic context and conditional funding-assurance relevance only; they do not evidence formal approval support objection consent no-role funding decision or transferred/concurrent WPL function

decision: Control WECA/MCA current-law function map while retaining no-role consent and funding dependency blockers
options_considered: Treat scoped-source silence as no-role; leave WECA/MCA function map entirely open; state only that scoped sources do not support transferred/concurrent WPL order-making
rationale: Transport Act WPL sources frame local or joint licensing schemes by non-metropolitan local traffic authorities; acquired WECA/MCA current-law and constitution sources do not identify an express transferred or concurrent WPL order-making function but do show strategic funding and transport interfaces

decision: Control WECA/MCA public meeting-record search while retaining no-role consent and funding dependency blockers
options_considered: Treat bounded public search absence as no-role; ignore WPL-adjacent hits; classify official ModernGov hits and preserve funding-assurance interfaces
rationale: Stage 2G bounded official ModernGov search did not locate formal Bristol WPL approval support objection consent sponsorship or funding decision; material hits show public-item token matches and CRSTS TCR mass-transit investment-fund delivery-assurance context only

decision: Control WECA/MCA package funding and assurance triggers while retaining no-role consent consultation-response and final funding dependency blockers
options_considered: Misclassify CRSTS TCR mass-transit investment-fund and delivery-assurance material as WPL approval; leave funding assurance context unclassified; classify conditional package triggers and create bounded context packet
rationale: Stage 2H evidence supports conditional package-level funding assurance interface classification only and requires package cost-line funding assurance route decision-source and gate-effect rows before OBC/FBC reliance

decision: Control Bristol final order and submission decision-box wording while retaining final route P0 blocker
options_considered: Close final order-maker route from committee remit; use officer authentication as order-making authority; keep route open but specify Full Council committee and officer execution options
rationale: Local evidence supports Bristol-led working assumption and OBC/FBC committee route but does not expressly allocate final WPL licensing scheme order-making or Secretary of State submission; Stage 2I preserves P0 and adds mandatory decision-box signatory and confirmation-condition controls

decision: Control DfT procedural expectation and engagement classification while retaining WPL-specific process open
options_considered: Treat generic DfT business-case and TAG guidance as WPL confirmation procedure; treat GOV.UK search absence as proof no procedure exists; classify guidance and search evidence while preserving DfT process gap
rationale: Stage 2J evidence supports Secretary of State confirmation as statutory assumption and generic DfT TAG business-case alignment but does not identify a WPL-specific DfT dossier format contact route timetable inquiry modification or post-confirmation process

decision: Control WPL revocation variation and consultation terminology while retaining process gaps
options_considered: Close revocation route entirely; apply RPI-only variation exemption to all amendments; classify making variation revocation and consultation controls while preserving publication DfT process public-law consultation and Bristol route gaps
rationale: Transport Act section 183 supports making varying and revoking by order section 184 controls initial and ordinary variation confirmation and 2009 Regulations give only a narrow RPI-only variation exemption; section 185 maps consultation and inquiry powers but does not close process readiness

decision: Create bounded context controls for future legal governance statutory OBC FBC DfT WECA MCA order-change and consultation agents while retaining all substantive blockers
options_considered: Leave context scattered across Stage 2B to Stage 2K; require every agent to load the whole repo; create mandatory packet domain add-ons banned claims and escalation rules
rationale: Stage 2B to Stage 2K controls are now too numerous for safe default loading; a compact mandatory packet reduces hallucination risk and preserves P0 P1 blockers without adding new authority evidence

decision: Create boundary and parking-inventory control architecture while retaining the Stage 4 P0 blocker
options_considered: Create dummy boundary and parking data; leave Stage 4 entirely placeholder; create source-provenance topology canonical inventory DPIA and operations-linkage controls without live data
rationale: Stage 4 cannot progress without authoritative boundary and parking inventory evidence; control templates and spatial QA reduce false-evidence risk while preserving no-go status for appraisal consultation revenue enforcement and statutory schedule reliance

decision: Create options and appraisal control architecture while retaining the Stage 5 P0 blocker
options_considered: Create shortlist and BCR from templates; leave appraisal entirely placeholder; create OAR ASR ASST model-card benefits transferability uncertainty and reappraisal controls without model outputs
rationale: Stage 5 cannot progress without OAR agreed ASR completed ASST model cards outputs uncertainty and review; control templates and appraisal QA reduce false recommendation and false VFM risk while preserving no-go status for OBC FBC consultation and statutory reliance

decision: Create strategic assessment and package funding controls while retaining the Stage 3 no-go blocker
options_considered: Create preferred strategic case and investment package; leave strategic assessment entirely placeholder; create problem baseline objective theory-of-change benefits dependency and package funding controls without selecting a package
rationale: Stage 3 cannot progress without evidenced problem definition baseline objectives stakeholder evidence theory of change dependencies benefits and package funding assurance; control templates and strategic QA reduce false strategic-readiness risk while preserving no-go status for OBC FBC consultation and statutory reliance

decision: Create OBC readiness and assembly controls while retaining the OBC assembly no-go blocker
options_considered: Assemble a placeholder OBC from templates; leave assembly script passive; actively block assembly and create section dependency claim dependency no-go claim and assurance controls
rationale: Open P0 blockers make OBC assembly unsafe; an active assembly guard and OBC QA reduce false-readiness risk while preserving no-go status for OBC approval consultation FBC and statutory reliance

decision: Create consultation readiness controls while retaining the consultation launch no-go blocker
options_considered: Draft consultation material and questionnaire now; leave consultation area empty; create launch material stakeholder privacy accessibility response-data analysis and no-go controls only
rationale: Open legal WECA MCA DfT spatial appraisal OBC DPIA equality accessibility and operating-procedure blockers make launch and material production unsafe; Stage 8A controls reduce false consultation readiness risk while preserving no-go status

decision: Create public/officer assurance layer while retaining all substantive no-go blockers
options_considered: Leave README technical and agent-facing; create a public microsite only; restructure README and add public officer controls registers and validators
rationale: The repo needs to be understandable by public readers cabinet members officers and specialist reviewers without implying approval or readiness; Stage 9A improves navigation lessons and checks while preserving all P0 P1 blockers

decision: Create OBC assurance gate controls while retaining the Stage 7 OBC gate no-go blocker
options_considered: Try to pass Stage 7 gate now; leave Stage 7 undefined; create checklist panel decision-report and red-team controls without a proceed recommendation
rationale: Open legal WECA MCA boundary appraisal OBC evidence and consultation blockers make an OBC gate pass unsafe; Stage 7A defines the future gate test while preserving no-go status

decision: Create statutory dossier controls while retaining the statutory submission no-go blocker
options_considered: Rejected option: assemble a statutory submission now. Rejected option: leave statutory dossier placeholders unstructured. Selected option: create component readiness no-go route and clause controls without submission readiness.
rationale: Open Bristol WECA MCA DfT boundary consultation FBC finance operations and statutory route blockers make submission unsafe; Stage 10A defines the future dossier test while preserving no-go status

decision: Create final FBC/statutory gate controls while retaining the Stage 11 no-go blocker
options_considered: Rejected option: recommend FBC approval or statutory submission now. Rejected option: rely only on generic gate-obc/gate-fbc checks. Selected option: create final gate checklist assurance panel no-go decision-report and red-team controls without readiness.
rationale: Open legal WECA MCA DfT boundary consultation FBC Section 151 finance commercial operations data appraisal equality and residual-risk blockers make a recommendation unsafe; Stage 11A defines the future final gate test while preserving no-go status

decision: Record public repository release while retaining all WPL no-go blockers
options_considered: Rejected option: treat public GitHub visibility as an endorsement or readiness milestone. Selected option: create publication checklist no-go register scan register and public-release validator.
rationale: The repository is public but public visibility is not approval; Stage 12A records publication controls and keeps all WPL readiness gates blocked.

decision: Create critical-path handover controls while retaining all substantive no-go blockers
options_considered: Rejected option: treat the critical path as an approved programme. Rejected option: leave open blockers unowned. Selected option: map blockers to planning work packages and a control-only 90-day plan without approval spend procurement or gate effects.
rationale: Open legal WECA MCA DfT spatial appraisal consultation FBC finance operations data equality and public-release blockers make mobilisation unsafe; Stage 13A improves handover clarity while preserving every no-go status.

decision: Create source-note controls while retaining the acquired-source note backlog
options_considered: Rejected option: mark source notes complete for all priority sources. Rejected option: rely directly on extraction text without source-use notes. Selected option: create a cross-workstream source-note pilot coverage register no-go register and validator while keeping ISS-0007 open.
rationale: High-confidence business-case drafting is unsafe without source-use limits and reviewed notes; Stage 14A creates a repeatable pattern without claiming full evidence review or claim verification.
